question: What's the difference between the Decreasing Cover and Level Cover options for the Death Benefit?
reference_answer: Under Option A (Decreasing Cover), the Sum Assured decreases over the Period of Coverage in line with the loan repayment schedule as specified in the Certificate of Insurance. Under Option B (Level Cover), the Sum Assured remains constant throughout the Period of Coverage as chosen by the Master Policyholder or Member and specified in the Certificate of Insurance. The moratorium option is only available with Option A.

question: If a 68-year-old borrower with Option A (Decreasing Cover) dies during the moratorium period, how much does the nominee receive?
reference_answer: The member is eligible for coverage as they are between 15 and 69 years of age at entry. If the moratorium option was chosen (available only with Option A in multiples of 3 months, minimum 3 months), then upon death during the moratorium period and claim approval, the Company shall pay the Sum Assured as specified in the Certificate of Insurance, irrespective of the actual loan outstanding. After the moratorium period expires, the Sum Assured would decrease over the remainder of the Period of Coverage.

question: My father is 72 and needs a loan. The bank requires this insurance — can he get covered?
reference_answer: No. Eligibility requires being between 15 and 69 years of age on the Date of Commencement of Risk. At 72, your father exceeds the maximum entry age of 69. Additionally, he must not be more than 70 years of age on the date the Insurance expires. The proposed Period of Coverage must be at least 1 year.

question: If a member paid a premium of ₹10,000 and surrenders after 24 months of a 60-month coverage period with Level Cover Sum Assured of ₹5,00,000, what is the surrender value?
reference_answer: Using the surrender value formula: Surrender Value = 70% of Premium paid x (Unexpired Period of Coverage in months / Total Period of Coverage in months) x (Sum Assured on surrender date / Sum Assured on commencement). Unexpired period = 60 - 24 = 36 months. For Level Cover, the Sum Assured ratio is 1. So: 0.70 x ₹10,000 x (36/60) x 1 = ₹4,200.

question: My mother passed away two months ago — I just found her insurance certificate. Can we still file a claim?
reference_answer: The notification deadline has likely passed. The Master Policyholder must notify Max Life Insurance in writing within 30 days from the date of the insured event. Since two months (approximately 60 days) have elapsed, the prescribed notification period has been exceeded. However, you should still contact the Claims Unit at Max Life Insurance Company Limited, Plot 90A, Sector 18, HUDA, Gurugram 122002, Haryana, India, as the Company may consider late claims under special circumstances, though acceptance is not guaranteed.

question: I've been paying premiums for 3 years on a 7-year plan with a level Sum Assured of ₹5,00,000 and total premium of ₹10,000. I want to stop. Will the company give me anything?
reference_answer: Yes, you can surrender the policy at any time by making a written request through the Master Policyholder. The Surrender Value is calculated as: 70% of Premium paid multiplied by (Unexpired Period of Coverage in months / Total Period of Coverage in months) multiplied by (Sum Assured applicable on the date of Surrender / Sum Assured on the Date of Commencement of Risk). For a 7-year (84-month) plan with 3 years (36 months) paid, the unexpired period is 48 months. For Level Cover the Sum Assured ratio is 1. So: 0.70 x ₹10,000 x (48/84) x 1 = ₹4,000.

question: I'm gathering paperwork for a claim after my husband's death. I have the death certificate but can't find the policy document. What else do I need?
reference_answer: You need several additional documents beyond the death certificate: (i) a claimant's statement in the prescribed form; (ii) the original Certificate of Insurance (if you cannot find your copy, contact the Master Policyholder to obtain a duplicate); (iii) identity proof of the Member (your husband) and the Nominee(s) bearing their photographs and signatures; and (iv) any other documents or information required by the Company for assessing and approving the claim request. The Master Policyholder must also notify Max Life Insurance in writing within 30 days of the death.

question: Under what circumstances can Max Life Insurance repudiate or cancel a claim or policy?
reference_answer: The Company can repudiate a claim or cancel the policy in several circumstances: (1) if the age declared is found to be incorrect, the Company may cancel and pay Surrender Value or adjust benefits (Section 9.5); (2) concealment, non-disclosure, misrepresentation, or fraud renders the policy voidable, and the Company may cancel and pay Surrender Value once fraud is established under Section 45 of the Insurance Act (Section 9.6.2); (3) if policy conditions are contravened or untrue averments are made, the Insurance ceases and the policy may be cancelled (Section 9.6.3); (4) if a fraudulent claim is made, the policy becomes void (Section 9.10); (5) the Company may also scrutinize documents and investigate the cause of death to repudiate partially or completely (Section 5.3).

question: What happens if the group policy is cancelled?
reference_answer: Either the Master Policyholder or the Company may terminate this Policy by giving 90 days prior written notice to the other party. Upon termination, each Member's coverage continues as an individual policy until the date of expiration of the Period of Coverage. No new enrollment forms will be accepted from the date of termination.

question: My colleague is turning 70 next month and his loan application requires this policy. Will he be accepted?
reference_answer: Your colleague may be eligible if he is currently 69 years of age (not more than 69 on the Date of Commencement of Risk). However, he must also not be more than 70 years of age on the date the Insurance expires. Since he is turning 70 next month, eligibility depends on whether the coverage period would extend past his 70th birthday. If the policy starts before he turns 70 and the Period of Coverage ends before he turns 71 (at least 1 year), he should qualify. If the policy period would extend beyond his 70th birthday, he does not meet the age condition.
